descEn: Bigger binary numbers — like a real computer!

teacherGuide.conceptGu: Advanced binary — bigger values. 8-bit thinking intro. Real computers use 8, 16, 32, 64 bits. Show connection: 8 bits = 1 byte = smallest unit of computer memory.
teacherGuide.conceptEn: 8-bit numbers, intro to bytes. Connect to real computer memory.
teacherGuide.storyGu: 'Yuval, computer memory = billions of bits! 8 bits = 1 byte. 1 photo = millions of bytes! Chess story: doubling — 64 squares = 64 bits is huge number!'
teacherGuide.scriptGu: 'Big numbers same way — more boxes, more power. 10000 in binary = 16! Computer thinks fast because bits flip in nanoseconds!'
teacherGuide.tipsGu: 💡 Count something big — how many bits needed? Age 7 = 3 bits. Age 100 needs 7 bits!

questions.hintEn: 8 bits = 1 Byte — the building block of all computer memory!
